contextBlurb: Saskatchewan's housing legislative activity includes Bill No. 608, The Rent Control Act, 2025, introduced by April ChiefCalf, and Bill No. 45, The Co-operatives Consequential Amendments Act, 2025, which amends The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006. The province also passed The Saskatchewan Affordability Act in December 2024 and has ongoing programs under the Saskatchewan Housing Action Plan 2025-2028 and the Canada Housing Infrastructure Fund.

legislation.title: Social Services Budget – Affordable Housing Repairs and New Units (2026-27)
legislation.summary: A $17.6 million provincial budget commitment in 2026-27 to support repairs in provincially owned housing units and new affordable housing projects.

legislation.title: Canada Housing Infrastructure Fund – Provincial-Territorial Stream (Fall 2025 Intake)
legislation.summary: A cost-shared federal-provincial funding program for infrastructure projects supporting housing in Saskatchewan communities, with applications accepted until November 28, 2025.

legislation.title: Canada Housing Infrastructure Fund Provincial-Territorial Stream – Fall 2025 Intake
legislation.summary: A federal-provincial cost-shared funding program under which Saskatchewan communities and eligible organizations applied for infrastructure funding to support housing. Applications closed November 28, 2025.

legislation.title: The Safer Communities and Neighbourhoods Amendment Act, 2024
legislation.summary: Amends the Safer Communities and Neighbourhoods Act, receiving royal assent in December 2024 and coming into force April 29–30, 2025.

legislation.title: The Saskatchewan Housing Action Plan 2025-2028
legislation.summary: A provincial plan outlining housing strategies and targets for affordable and low-income housing development across Saskatchewan from 2025 to 2028.

legislation.title: The Saskatchewan Affordability Act
legislation.summary: Legislation enacted in December 2024 addressing affordability measures for Saskatchewan residents.

legislation.title: The Saskatchewan Affordability Act
legislation.summary: Legislation enacted in December 2024 to address affordability concerns in Saskatchewan. Received Royal Assent on December 10, 2024.

legislation.title: The Co-operatives Consequential Amendments Act, 2025
legislation.summary: An act making consequential amendments resulting from co-operatives legislation, including an amendment to The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 to allow conversion of residential property into a housing co-operative.

legislation.title: The Rent Control Act, 2025
legislation.summary: A private member's bill introduced by April ChiefCalf relating to rent control in Saskatchewan.
